pattern: Favorite Drops Points, Group Volatility Rises
description: When a group favorite draws or loses early, the group becomes more volatile because challengers can realistically compete for the top position. This increases the causal importance of later head-to-head matches.
Back-to-back wins without conceding usually signal more than points accumulation. They show tactical control, defensive reliability, and reduced volatility across the next fixtures.

pattern: Dropped Points Convert the Next Match Into a Pressure Fixture
description: When a favorite opens with a draw while a rival wins, the next match carries higher psychological and table pressure. The match becomes less about routine qualification and more about restoring control.

pattern: Statement Win Creates Narrative and Table Leverage
description: A large-margin win creates two forms of leverage at once: practical goal-difference advantage and narrative momentum. This can change how later opponents, analysts, and fans evaluate the team.

pattern: Heavy Win Converts Outsider Into Dark Horse
description: When a non-favorite wins heavily, the result can shift public interpretation faster than a narrow win. The team gains table leverage, confidence, and narrative visibility.
